era: 2010s-present. Seattle. Indie Rock / Garage Rock. DIY Underground. E=5. Post-streak reset (first degree after Capitol Hill six-degree streak 110-115). Self-released. Duo.

location.formative: Seattle's DIY underground. House shows, basement venues, all-ages spaces. The network below the club circuit. The permanent underground that sustains the scene.
location.career: Pony Time -- indie rock/garage rock duo. 2010s-present. Self-released. Luke Beetham + Stacy Peck. Seattle DIY.

associated_musicians.name: Pretty Girls Make Graves
associated_musicians.note: Post-Punk (115, E=5, Matador, four-piece, elegant) vs. Garage Rock (116, E=5, self-released, duo, raw). Architecture vs. impulse. Streak closer vs. post-streak reset.

associated_musicians.name: Kinski
associated_musicians.note: Kinski: maximalist (extended, layered, hypnotic). Pony Time: minimalist (short, stripped, immediate). Both E=5. Accumulation vs. reduction.

associated_musicians.name: Veronica Lipgloss
associated_musicians.note: Both punk/garage-adjacent. Veronica Lipgloss E=6; Pony Time E=5. Similar rawness, different intensity.

associated_musicians.name: Mudhoney
associated_musicians.note: Both garage rock at E=5. Mudhoney on Sub Pop; Pony Time self-released. Same energy, same genre territory, different decades.

associated_musicians.name: Nirvana
associated_musicians.note: Nirvana began in the DIY underground before Sub Pop before DGC. Pony Time stays in the DIY underground. The origin that Nirvana left.

theory_nodes.concept: The raw transmission (one hundred seventeenth acoustic element -- simplest possible rock unit delivering music with no mediation; duo minimalism; overdriven immediacy; short-form delivery; lo-fi honesty; the signal chain is instrument to amplifier to room to listener)
examples: Pony Time (degree 116) -- garage rock duo. Guitar + drums + vocals. E=5. No mediation.
Contrast with the burning elegance (degree 115, Pretty Girls Make Graves) -- elegant architecture. The raw transmission is anti-architecture.
Contrast with the hypnotic engine (degree 110, Kinski) -- extended, patient. The raw transmission is short, urgent.
Contrast with the collapse frequency (degree 109, No-Anchor) -- structured collapse. The raw transmission has no structure complex enough to collapse.
theory_nodes.description: The raw transmission is the 117th acoustic element. Minimal garage rock duo delivering music with no mediation.

ancestors: The burning elegance (degree 115) -- architecture. The raw transmission is anti-architecture.
The punk circuit (degree 108) -- explosive. The raw transmission is similarly raw but more minimal.
genealogy.relationship: One hundred seventeen acoustic elements across one hundred sixteen degrees. Track element 118 at degree 117.

theory_nodes.concept: The post-streak reset (one hundred seventeenth artist-city pattern -- long streak followed by dramatic simplification; genre shifts from elaborate to minimal; label shifts from prestigious to self-released; format shifts from full band to duo; energy holds while everything else resets)
examples: Capitol Hill streak (110-115, six degrees) followed by Pony Time (116, self-released duo).
Genre: Post-Punk (115) to Garage Rock (116). Label: Matador (115) to Self-Released (116).
Format: four-piece (115) to duo (116). Production: full (115) to lo-fi (116).
Energy holds at E=5. Everything else resets. The engine strips away complexity.
theory_nodes.description: The post-streak reset is the 117th artist-city pattern. Long streaks end with dramatic simplification.

ancestors: Capitol Hill streak (110-115). Six consecutive. All-time record. Followed by reset at 116.
genealogy.relationship: One hundred seventeen artist-city patterns across one hundred sixteen degrees. Track pattern 118 at degree 117.

theory_nodes.concept: Self-Released as the DIY absolute -- the extreme end of the independence spectrum; no label, no distribution, no A&R; music released through Bandcamp and physical media at shows; the permanent underground that sustains the scene
examples: Pony Time (degree 116) -- fully self-released. No label at all.
Label spectrum: Major (DGC) -> Independent (Sub Pop) -> Indie Prestige (Matador) -> Self-Released (Pony Time).
First fully self-released artist in recent engine history.
The DIY tradition: cassettes, 7-inches, CDRs, Bandcamp. Music that exists whether or not anyone is paying attention.
theory_nodes.description: Self-Released at degree 116. The DIY absolute. No label. The permanent underground.

ancestors: Sub Pop (most recurring label). Matador (degree 115). Self-Released is the opposite end.

theory_nodes.concept: Duo minimalism -- two-piece format (guitar + drums) as a declaration of minimalism; no bass, no keyboards, no samples; connects to White Stripes, early Black Keys, Lightning Bolt; Pony Time's approach is more direct -- no effects, no trickery
examples: Pony Time (degree 116) -- Luke Beetham (guitar/vocals) + Stacy Peck (drums/vocals).
Two-piece lineage: White Stripes, early Black Keys, Lightning Bolt, Death From Above 1979.
The duo format is not a limitation -- it is a declaration. Everything you hear is two people in a room.
Antithetical to the extended-form, atmospheric approaches of the Capitol Hill streak.
theory_nodes.description: Duo minimalism at degree 116. Two-piece format. Guitar + drums. Declaration of minimalism.

ancestors: Four-piece format (degree 115, Pretty Girls Make Graves). Duo is the reduction.
genealogy.relationship: Duo format at degree 116. Track whether other duos appear.

cross_references.relationship: CONSECUTIVE CONTRAST. Post-Punk (115, Matador, four-piece, elegant) vs. Garage Rock (116, self-released, duo, raw). Same E=5. Architecture vs. impulse.
MINIMALISM SPECTRUM. Kinski: maximalist (extended, layered, hypnotic). Pony Time: minimalist (short, stripped, immediate). Both E=5.
GARAGE ADJACENCY. Both punk/garage-adjacent. Veronica Lipgloss E=6; Pony Time E=5. Similar rawness, different intensity.
GARAGE LINEAGE. Both garage rock at E=5. Mudhoney on Sub Pop; Pony Time self-released. Same energy, different decades.
DIY ORIGINS. Nirvana began in the DIY underground before Sub Pop before DGC. Pony Time stays there. The origin that Nirvana left.
